executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with 101 Canadian organizations compromised in a single 48-hour period. The Qilin ransomware group leads the attack wave with 21 victims, followed by CLOP (13), Play (11), and SafePay (11). High-profile targets include Canada Goose (consumer retail), National Money Mart (financial services with database leaked), Eastern Townships School Board (education), Peterborough Public Health, Municipality of North Perth, and MRC de Maskinongé (public sector). Critical infrastructure sectors hit hardest include manufacturing (21), technology (16), construction (11), business services (11), and financial services (9). No major Canadian banks (RBC, TD, CIBC, Scotiabank, BMO) or large retailers (Loblaws, Canadian Tire) were directly named, though Money Mart's breach represents significant financial sector exposure.

items.name: Qilin (also known as Agenda)
items.targets: 21 Canadian victims: Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, A Lococo Wholesale, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Bergmanis Preyra LLP, Pre-Con Builders, Questica, Eastern Townships School Board, MG CPA, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), Maheu&Maheu, Berts Electric, Capp Shupak. Sectors: Transportation, Manufacturing, Technology, Construction, Public Sector, Education, Hospitality, Energy, Healthcare, Business Services.
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application). Double-extortion ransomware with public leak site.

items.name: CLOP (also known as Cl0p)
items.targets: 13 Canadian victims: Strategic Objectives, Ideal Welders, Conwest, CMH Heli, Granite Pointe, SMT Lawyers, Cloud Data Works, Sensible Solutions, Whiskijack Resorts, CCCM.BC.CA, Technikel, Modtech, Eastplats, Centaur Products. Sectors: Business Services, Manufacturing, Transportation, Hospitality, Legal, Technology, Energy.
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service). Known for mass-exploitation campaigns targeting file transfer vulnerabilities (MOVEit, GoAnywhere). All targets listed in uppercase suggest automated victim listing.

items.name: Play
items.targets: 11 Canadian victims: Select Tool, Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Paisley Products, Makivik, ALLMAX Nutrition, Pewarchuk CPA, Security ONE Alarm, Katch Kan, Turkstra Trusses. Sectors: Manufacturing, Agriculture, Technology, Financial Services, Business Services.
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter), T1083 (File and Directory Discovery). Known for targeting Windows and Linux systems, deleting shadow copies.

items.name: SafePay
items.targets: 11 Canadian victims: Briway Carriers, Matt and Steve, RIPOBEC, Groupe Piche, Precision Aluminum, Kenalex, Silverline Group, Teccart College, MacTavish Co. Sectors: Transportation, Agriculture, Construction, Manufacturing, Education, Consumer Services.
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service). Emerging ransomware group with focus on mid-sized Canadian businesses.

items.name: DragonForce
items.targets: 6 Canadian victims: Phoenix Labs, CCR Solutions, Esposito Bros Construction, Soteck, McKee-Pownall Equine Services, Emond Publishing. Sectors: Technology, Construction, Agriculture, Healthcare, Education.
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application).

items.name: TheGentlemen (also known as The Gentlemen Ransomware)
items.targets: 5 Canadian victims: Groupe Caddac, ControlGMC, Labtician Ophthalmics, Real Tech, Colliers. Sectors: Construction, Manufacturing, Healthcare, Business Services.
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery).

items.name: Lynx
items.targets: 5 Canadian victims: Peterborough Public Health, Laurysen Kitchens, Group Echo, KTL Group, Trucash. Sectors: Public Sector, Manufacturing, Business Services, Technology, Financial Services.
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service). Notable for targeting public health infrastructure.

items.name: IncRansom
items.targets: 7 Canadian victims: CPK Interior, Excavation Tourigny, Glassers TV, Steelworks Inc, Bison Family Medical, Omega Tool Corp, Reidhurst Nagy, Bais Yaakov. Sectors: Manufacturing, Construction, Consumer Services, Healthcare, Financial Services, Education.
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery).

items.name: Sinobi
items.targets: 3 Canadian victims: Active Green + Ross, Morison Insurance Brokers, Kids & Company. Sectors: Consumer Services, Financial Services.
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service).

items.name: AiLock
items.targets: 2 Canadian victims: Solutions Extreme Technology, Eeyou Communications Network. Sectors: Technology, Telecommunication.
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery).

items.name: Devman
items.targets: 4 Canadian victims: Beausejour Co-op, Axion50plus (2 entries), Ravand. Sectors: Agriculture, Financial Services, Technology.
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service).

items.name: Nightspire
items.targets: 2 Canadian victims: Regal Building Materials, HO** RIL** LLP. Sectors: Construction.
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact).

items.name: Akira
items.targets: 1 Canadian victim: Inland Audio Visual. Sector: Business Services.
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1566 (Phishing), T1078 (Valid Accounts). Known for targeting VPN vulnerabilities.

items.name: Everest
items.targets: 1 Canadian victim: National Money Mart Company - DATABASE LEAKED. Sector: Financial Services. Critical breach with confirmed data exfiltration.
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1005 (Data from Local System). Double-extortion with confirmed database leak.

items.name: Medusa
items.targets: 1 Canadian victim: MRC de Maskinongé district (municipal government). Sector: Public Sector.
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery).

items.name: MedusaLocker
items.targets: 1 Canadian victim: Dulay. Sector: Technology.
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1112 (Modify Registry).

items.name: Nitrogen
items.targets: 1 Canadian victim: Whitfield Welding. Sector: Manufacturing.
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact).

items.name: Bravox
items.targets: 1 Canadian victim: John O's Foods. Sector: Agriculture and Food Production.
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact).

items.name: Securotrop
items.targets: 2 Canadian victims: Living Realty, Cadman Power Equipment. Sectors: Business Services, Manufacturing.
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact).

items.name: Worldleaks
items.targets: 1 Canadian victim: Municipality of North Perth. Sector: Public Sector (municipal government).
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service).

items.name: Anubis
items.targets: 1 Canadian victim: One law firm (unnamed). Sector: Business Services (Legal).
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact).

items.name: Payload
items.targets: 1 Canadian victim: Q2 Artificial Lift Services. Sector: Manufacturing.
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact).

items.name: CoinbaseCartel
items.targets: 1 Canadian victim: Canada Goose (major luxury apparel brand). Sector: Consumer Services.
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service).

canada_landscape.summary: Canada experienced a massive coordinated ransomware campaign with 101 organizations breached across all provinces in 48 hours. Critical infrastructure heavily impacted: 3 municipal governments (North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé, Peterborough Public Health), 1 school board (Eastern Townships), and 1 telecommunications provider (Eeyou Communications Network). No CCCS advisories were issued in the reporting period despite the scale of attacks. Manufacturing (21 victims) and technology (16 victims) sectors were primary targets.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (luxury apparel manufacturer) confirmed compromised by CoinbaseCartel ransomware. Active Green + Ross (automotive service retail chain) compromised by Sinobi ransomware. Kids & Company (childcare services) compromised by Sinobi ransomware.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (payday loan and financial services) suffered critical breach by Everest ransomware with DATABASE LEAKED notation indicating confirmed data exfiltration. Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi. Trucash (financial technology) compromised by Lynx. Multiple accounting firms breached: Pewarchuk CPA (Play), MG CPA (Qilin), Reidhurst Nagy (IncRansom). No breaches of major Canadian banks (RBC, TD, CIBC, Scotiabank, BMO) identified.

report_2w.executive_summary: A critical authentication bypass vulnerability in cPanel/WHM (CVE-2026-41940) is being actively exploited in the wild, affecting 1.5 million servers globally. Canadian authorities (CCCS) have issued multiple alerts including one for this cPanel flaw, a Linux kernel vulnerability providing root access (CVE-2026-31431), and ongoing social engineering attacks targeting enterprise cloud services. The threat actor ShinyHunters has been on a breach spree, hitting multiple organizations including education technology vendors in coordinated supply chain attacks. Nation-state actors continue targeting critical infrastructure, with TeamPCP compromising six software ecosystems and North Korean Lazarus Group leveraging AI for cyber operations.

items.description: Critical authentication bypass in cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM)
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Trivial exploit providing root access to Linux kernel
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Vulnerability impacting Cisco ASA and FTD devices
Vulnerability impacting Cisco ASA and FTD devices
Vulnerability impacting Cisco ASA and FTD devices
Apache path traversal vulnerability being actively scanned
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: Medium, Availability: Low

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Aman luxury hotels, Marcus & Millichap real estate, Towerpoint Wealth LLC, Follett Software (EdTech), multiple Salesforce instances
items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1213 (Data from Information Repositories), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - targeting Salesforce environments and luxury/financial sectors

items.name: TeamPCP
items.targets: Ruby Gems, Go Modules, NPM packages, SAP npm packages - cross-platform supply chain
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Compromise Software Supply Chain), T1071 (Application Layer Protocol) - compromised six software ecosystems

items.name: Qilin Ransomware
items.targets: Zinkan & Barker Development, Eduporium (educational services), multiple unverified claims
items.ttps: T1021.001 (Remote Desktop Protocol), T1082 (System Information Discovery) - uses stealthy RDP history enumeration to map victim networks

items.name: Lazarus Group (North Korea)
items.targets: Federal agencies, cryptocurrency platforms, general cybersecurity targeting
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter) - leveraging AI for cybersecurity operations

items.name: CORDIAL SPIDER / SNARKY SPIDER
items.targets: Enterprise SaaS environments via SSO exploitation
items.ttps: T1539 (Steal Web Session Cookie), T1556 (Modify Authentication Process), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - high-speed SaaS attacks using AiTM and SSO exploitation

items.name: 313 Team (pro-Iran)
items.targets: Ubuntu.com DDoS attack
items.ttps: T1498 (Network Denial of Service), T1583 (Acquire Infrastructure) - politically motivated DDoS campaigns

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued four critical alerts in the past two weeks covering the cPanel authentication bypass (CVE-2026-41940), Linux kernel root escalation (CVE-2026-31431), Cisco ASA/FTD vulnerabilities, and social engineering attacks targeting enterprise SaaS environments. A Canadian Senate report warns that Russia is actively targeting Canada with disinformation campaigns. No direct incidents involving major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in this period.
